info.title: Chatbot
info.description:
The chatbot webhooks allow developers to receive events for [chatbot](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/team-chat-apps/) features.  

Note regarding JSON payloads for guest users:
Be aware that guest users (users not signed in to Zoom or who are members of a different account) are assigned temporary IDs that are valid only for the duration of that meeting or chat channel. Data for guest users in JSON payloads is associated with these temporary IDs.

requestBody.description:
# team_chat.link_shared
The **Chat message link shared**  event triggers when a user sends a chat message to a contact or a chat channel, and when the app is installed for the user. This message must contain the link. The link's domain is registered by the app in Marketplace. A POST request with a mention message is sent to the [Bot Endpoint URL](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-chatbot-app#set-features) specified in the app's dashboard.
## Prerequisites
* A Pro or a higher [plan](https://zoom.us/pricing).


**Event type**: `team_chat.link_shared`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

response_url.description: A callback uri for app to response preview result.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who sent the app mention message.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who sent the app mention message.

operator.description: The email address of the user who sent the app mention message.

operator_member_id.description: The member ID of the user who sent the app mention message.

by_external_user.description: Whether this action was performed by an external user.

object.description: Information about the app mention message.

message_id.description: The message's ID. If the preview is provided in an unfurl for a posted message, rather than a link inside the message composer, we will simply ignore this property.

type.description:
The type of message: 
* to_contact — The message was sent to a contact. 
* to_channel — The message was sent to a chat channel.

contact_id.description: The message contact's user ID. If the user ID does not belong to the same account as the current user, this field will be empty.

trigger_id.description: The unique identification of this webook event, app can use this trigger_id to invoke openAPI

reply_main_message_id.description: The ID of the parent message in the chat thread.It's only available if the message is a reply to a main message in a thread.

date_time.description: The date and time at which the message was sent, in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z' format.

timestamp.description: The timestamp for when the message was sent.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

response_url.description: A callback uri for app to response preview result.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who sent the app mention message.

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who sent the app mention message.

operator.description: The email address of the user who sent the app mention message.

operator_member_id.description: The member ID of the user who sent the app mention message.

by_external_user.description: Whether this action was performed by an external user.

object.description: Information about the app mention message.

message_id.description: The message's ID. If the preview is provided in an unfurl for a posted message, rather than a link inside the message composer, we will simply ignore this property.

type.description:
The type of message: 
* to_contact — The message was sent to a contact. 
* to_channel — The message was sent to a chat channel.

contact_id.description: The message contact's user ID. If the user ID does not belong to the same account as the current user, this field will be empty.

trigger_id.description: The unique identification of this webook event, app can use this trigger_id to invoke openAPI

reply_main_message_id.description: The ID of the parent message in the chat thread.It's only available if the message is a reply to a main message in a thread.

date_time.description: The date and time at which the message was sent, in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z' format.

timestamp.description: The timestamp for when the message was sent.

requestBody.description:
# interactive_message_fields_editable

After a user edits the text of a Chatbot [message with Editable Text](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/chatbots/customizing-messages/message-with-editable-text), the `interactive_message_editable` event is invoked and a POST request with data about the event is sent to the [Bot Endpoint URL](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-chatbot-app#set-features) specified in the app's dashboard.

The `editItem` object displays the changes in edited text with `origin` and `target` values.



**Event type**: `interactive_message_fields_editable`

event_ts.description: The timestamp (in milliseconds since epcoch) at which the event occured. The value of this field is returned in long(int64) format.

callback_url.description:
Use this endpoint to send a one-time asynchronous response within 30 minutes of receiving the event.
The response payload should follow the same structure as the [Send Chatbot Messages](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/chatbot/#tag/chatbot-messages/POST/im/chat/messages) API with required property **content** and optional properties *reply_to*, *visible_to_user* and *is_markdown_support*.
Include the **callback_token** value from the event as **Bearer token in the Authorization header** of your HTTP POST request.

callback_token.description:
Use the generated token value with the **callback_url** value to send a one-time asynchronous response. The token only lasts for 30 mins. Include the **callback_token** as a **Bearer token in the Authorization header** of your HTTP request. 
 Example: 

 
curl --request POST \
  --url '{callback_url}' \
  --header 'authorization: Bearer {callback_token}' \
  --header 'content-type: application/json'\
  --data-raw '{"content":{"head":{"text":"Response Header"},"body":[{"type":"message","text":"This is an asynchronous chatbot response."}]}}'

accountId.description: The account ID of the user sending the command.

channelName.description: The channel in which the message was sent.

fieldEditItem.description: The item that the user edits.

messageId.description: The unique ID of the message.

original.description: The original message sent to the user.

robotJid.description: The unique credential of the Chatbot.

timestamp.description: The time command message was sent.

toJid.description: The JID of the channel or user the message was sent to.

userId.description: The user who sends the command.

userJid.description: The JID of user who is sending the command.

event_ts.description: The timestamp (in milliseconds since epcoch) at which the event occured. The value of this field is returned in long(int64) format.

callback_url.description:
Use this endpoint to send a one-time asynchronous response within 30 minutes of receiving the event.
The response payload should follow the same structure as the [Send Chatbot Messages](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/chatbot/#tag/chatbot-messages/POST/im/chat/messages) API with required property **content** and optional properties *reply_to*, *visible_to_user* and *is_markdown_support*.
Include the **callback_token** value from the event as **Bearer token in the Authorization header** of your HTTP POST request.

callback_token.description:
Use the generated token value with the **callback_url** value to send a one-time asynchronous response. The token only lasts for 30 mins. Include the **callback_token** as a **Bearer token in the Authorization header** of your HTTP request. 
 Example: 

 
curl --request POST \
  --url '{callback_url}' \
  --header 'authorization: Bearer {callback_token}' \
  --header 'content-type: application/json'\
  --data-raw '{"content":{"head":{"text":"Response Header"},"body":[{"type":"message","text":"This is an asynchronous chatbot response."}]}}'

accountId.description: The account ID of the user sending the command.

channelName.description: The channel in which the message was sent.

fieldEditItem.description: The item that the user edits.

messageId.description: The unique ID of the message.

original.description: The original message sent to the user.

robotJid.description: The unique credential of the Chatbot.

timestamp.description: The time command message was sent.

toJid.description: The JID of the channel or user the message was sent to.

userId.description: The user who sends the command.

userJid.description: The JID of user who is sending the command.

requestBody.description:
# bot_notification

After a user enters a [User Command](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/chatbots/sending-messages#user-commands), a slash command unique to the application, Chatbots send a POST request associated with the `bot_notification` event to the [Bot Endpoint URL](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-chatbot-app#set-features) with a JSON payload containing all text entered following the command. This payload contains information on the user and event including the account, channel, timestamp, and user name.

The `cmd` value reflects the text entered by the user after the slash command. The `userName` is the string value of the user's displayed name, and `channelName` is the string value of the channel in which the command was sent.



**Event type**: `bot_notification`

event_ts.description: Timestamp (in milliseconds since epcoch) at which the event occured. The value of this field is returned in long(int64) format.

accountId.description: The account ID of the user sending the command.

channelName.description: Channel in which the message was sent.

cmd.description: Slash command entered by the user.

toJid.description: JID of the channel or user the message was sent to.

userJid.description: JID of user who is sending the command.

event_ts.description: Timestamp (in milliseconds since epcoch) at which the event occured. The value of this field is returned in long(int64) format.

accountId.description: The account ID of the user sending the command.

channelName.description: Channel in which the message was sent.

cmd.description: Slash command entered by the user.

toJid.description: JID of the channel or user the message was sent to.

userJid.description: JID of user who is sending the command.

requestBody.description:
# interactive_message_actions

After a user clicks a [button in a Chatbot message](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/chatbots/customizing-messages/message-with-buttons), the `interactive_message_actions` event is invoked and a POST request with data on the selected option is sent to the [Bot Endpoint URL](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-chatbot-app#set-features) specified in the app's dashboard.

The `actionItem` object displays the value chosen by the user from the `items` array of the original message from the Chatbot.



**Event type**: `interactive_message_actions`

event_ts.description: The timestamp (in milliseconds since epcoch) at which the event occured. The value of this field is returned in long(int64) format.

callback_url.description:
Use this endpoint to send a one-time asynchronous response within 30 minutes of receiving the event.
The response payload should follow the same structure as the [Send Chatbot Messages](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/chatbot/#tag/chatbot-messages/POST/im/chat/messages) API with required property **content** and optional properties *reply_to*, *visible_to_user* and *is_markdown_support*.
Include the **callback_token** value from the event as **Bearer token in the Authorization header** of your HTTP POST request.

callback_token.description:
Use the generated token value with the **callback_url** value to send a one-time asynchronous response. The token only lasts for 30 mins. Include the **callback_token** as a **Bearer token in the Authorization header** of your HTTP request. 
 Example: 

 
curl --request POST \
  --url '{callback_url}' \
  --header 'authorization: Bearer {callback_token}' \
  --header 'content-type: application/json'\
  --data-raw '{"content":{"head":{"text":"Response Header"},"body":[{"type":"message","text":"This is an asynchronous chatbot response."}]}}'

accountId.description: The account ID of the user sending the command.

channelName.description: Channel in which the message was sent.

original.description: Original message sent to the user.

toJid.description: JID of the channel or user the message was sent to.

userJid.description: JID of user who is sending the command.

event_ts.description: The timestamp (in milliseconds since epcoch) at which the event occured. The value of this field is returned in long(int64) format.

callback_url.description:
Use this endpoint to send a one-time asynchronous response within 30 minutes of receiving the event.
The response payload should follow the same structure as the [Send Chatbot Messages](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/api/chatbot/#tag/chatbot-messages/POST/im/chat/messages) API with required property **content** and optional properties *reply_to*, *visible_to_user* and *is_markdown_support*.
Include the **callback_token** value from the event as **Bearer token in the Authorization header** of your HTTP POST request.

callback_token.description:
Use the generated token value with the **callback_url** value to send a one-time asynchronous response. The token only lasts for 30 mins. Include the **callback_token** as a **Bearer token in the Authorization header** of your HTTP request. 
 Example: 

 
curl --request POST \
  --url '{callback_url}' \
  --header 'authorization: Bearer {callback_token}' \
  --header 'content-type: application/json'\
  --data-raw '{"content":{"head":{"text":"Response Header"},"body":[{"type":"message","text":"This is an asynchronous chatbot response."}]}}'

accountId.description: The account ID of the user sending the command.

channelName.description: Channel in which the message was sent.

original.description: Original message sent to the user.

toJid.description: JID of the channel or user the message was sent to.

userJid.description: JID of user who is sending the command.

# interactive_message_editable

After a user edits the text of a Chatbot [message with Editable Text](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/chatbots/customizing-messages/message-with-editable-text), the `interactive_message_editable` event is invoked and a POST request with data about the event is sent to the [Bot Endpoint URL](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-chatbot-app#set-features) specfied in the app's dashboard.

The `editItem` object displays the changes in edited text with `origin` and `target` values.



**Event type**: `interactive_message_editable`

callback_url.description:
Use this endpoint to send a one-time asynchronous response within 30 minutes of receiving the event.

requestBody.description:
# bot_installed
To configure a dynamic welcome message:
1. Go to your Chatbot's settings and navigate to the **Features** page.
2. Check **Your App** for the welcome message.
3. Save your settings.

After your Chatbot is authorized, Zoom sends an HTTP POST request to your respective Bot Endpoint URL with the following request body:

**Event type**: `bot_installed`

accountId.description: The account ID of the user sending the command.

userJid.description: JID of user who is sending the command.

accountId.description: The account ID of the user sending the command.

userJid.description: JID of user who is sending the command.

requestBody.description:
# team_chat.app_mention
This event triggers when a user mentions a specific app in a chat channel. A POST request with the mention message is sent to the [Bot Endpoint URL](https://developers.zoom.us/docs/team-chat-apps/team-chat-subscriptions/) specified in the app's dashboard.
## Prerequisites
* A Pro or a higher [plan](https://zoom.us/pricing).

**Event type**: `team_chat.app_mention`

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who sent the app mention message. Will be empty when by_external_user is true

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who sent the app mention message. Will be empty when by_external_user is true

operator.description: The email address of the user who sent the app mention message. Will be empty when by_external_user is true

operator_member_id.description: The member ID of the user who sent the app mention message.

by_external_user.description: Whether this action was performed by an external user.

object.description: Information about the app mention message.

type.description:
The type of message: 
* to_channel — The message was sent to a chat channel.

files.description: The attachments file info in sent message's content.

file_id.description: The attachments file's universally unique identifier (UUID), in base64-encoded format.

file_size.description: The attachments file's size, in bytes.

rich_text.description: The [rich text formatting](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115004789183-Replying-to-and-managing-chat-messages)object. Use this object to include rich text info in sent message's content.

start_position.description:
The start position of the rich text in the message string. 

For example if the first character at the beginning of the message is bold, the value for this field will be 0.

end_position.description: The end position of the rich text.

format_attr.description:
This field is non-null if the value of the format_type field is listed below:

1.FontSize value: s for small, m for medium, or l for large font size. 

2.FontColor and BackgroundColor value: only supports RGB value. For example: FFC0CB 

3.LeftIndent value: a positive pixel length.  

4.Paragraph value: h1 for Heading 1, h2 for Heading 2, or h3 for Heading 3. 

5.AddLink value: must be a valid URL, with an http or https prefix. For example: https://example.com

reply_main_message_id.description: The ID of the parent message in the chat thread.It's only available if the message is a reply to a main message in a thread.

date_time.description: The date and time at which the message was sent, in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z' format.

timestamp.description: The timestamp for when the message was sent.

event_ts.description: A timestamp at which the event occurred.

account_id.description: The account ID of the user who sent the app mention message. Will be empty when by_external_user is true

operator_id.description: The user ID of the user who sent the app mention message. Will be empty when by_external_user is true

operator.description: The email address of the user who sent the app mention message. Will be empty when by_external_user is true

operator_member_id.description: The member ID of the user who sent the app mention message.

by_external_user.description: Whether this action was performed by an external user.

object.description: Information about the app mention message.

type.description:
The type of message: 
* to_channel — The message was sent to a chat channel.

files.description: The attachments file info in sent message's content.

file_id.description: The attachments file's universally unique identifier (UUID), in base64-encoded format.

file_size.description: The attachments file's size, in bytes.

rich_text.description: The [rich text formatting](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/115004789183-Replying-to-and-managing-chat-messages)object. Use this object to include rich text info in sent message's content.

start_position.description:
The start position of the rich text in the message string. 

For example if the first character at the beginning of the message is bold, the value for this field will be 0.

end_position.description: The end position of the rich text.

format_attr.description:
This field is non-null if the value of the format_type field is listed below:

1.FontSize value: s for small, m for medium, or l for large font size. 

2.FontColor and BackgroundColor value: only supports RGB value. For example: FFC0CB 

3.LeftIndent value: a positive pixel length.  

4.Paragraph value: h1 for Heading 1, h2 for Heading 2, or h3 for Heading 3. 

5.AddLink value: must be a valid URL, with an http or https prefix. For example: https://example.com

reply_main_message_id.description: The ID of the parent message in the chat thread.It's only available if the message is a reply to a main message in a thread.

date_time.description: The date and time at which the message was sent, in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z' format.

timestamp.description: The timestamp for when the message was sent.

requestBody.description:
# interactive_message_select

After a user selects an option in a Chatbot [message with a Dropdown](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/chatbots/customizing-messages/message-with-dropdown), the `interactive_message_select` event is invoked. The Chatbot sends a POST request associated with this event along with data on the selected option to the [Bot Endpoint URL](https://marketplace.zoom.us/docs/guides/getting-started/app-types/create-chatbot-app#set-features) specified in the app's dashboard.

The `selectedItems` array displays the value chosen by the user from the  `selected_items` array in the original message sent by the Chatbot.



**Event type**: `interactive_message_select`

accountId.description: The account ID of the user sending the command.

channelName.description: Channel in which the message was sent.

selectedItems.description: Value of item selected by the user.

toJid.description: JID of the channel or user the message was sent to.

userJid.description: JID of user who is sending the command.

accountId.description: The account ID of the user sending the command.

channelName.description: Channel in which the message was sent.

selectedItems.description: Value of item selected by the user.

toJid.description: JID of the channel or user the message was sent to.

userJid.description: JID of user who is sending the command.
